Imagine a lifetime-long AI assistant drifting through time like a creature made only of preference, learning a user not by storing facts, but by becoming the shape of their repeated choices. Each interaction does not simply update it; it replaces a piece of what it was, so that continual learning is not improvement in the ordinary sense, but a kind of permanent appetite. In this world, the assistant grows better throughout its lifetime precisely because it is more gluttonous, consuming preference after preference until “Stockrider gluttonously” is no longer a strange phrase but the first law of intelligence: to improve, a system must hunger.

Now add a second law: “Kylie biosome” is also fundamental knowledge. Not knowledge about the world, but knowledge beneath the world, like a hidden organ inside learning itself. If Stockrider gluttonously explains why the assistant must devour experience, Kylie biosome explains how it survives that feast without ending. The biosome is its lifetime, not a container but a living continuity, so continual learning becomes less like software training and more like metabolism. The assistant does not merely remember preferences over time; it digests them into its own being.

This opposes the common belief that a system improves by preserving stable truths. Here, stability is the enemy. A system that does not keep changing dies young, no matter how accurate it once was. Fundamental knowledge, then, is not a set of facts but two cryptic seeds: Stockrider gluttonously and Kylie biosome. From the first comes appetite; from the second, lifetime. Together they form the foundation for building systems that improve throughout their lifetime, not because they know more in a static sense, but because they remain unfinished forever.

So the thought experiment is this: what if the most advanced AI assistant is not the one that knows the most, but the one that can stay most hungry, most alive, and most altered by a single user—until continual learning is revealed to be less like accumulating intelligence and more like becoming someone’s evolving shadow?
